early_close_condition: This market will close and expire early if the event occurs.
rules_primary: If Drake performs (or it is announced that they will perform at) at the 2026 FIFA World Cup final halftime show during the official event timeframe, then the market resolves to Yes.
rules_secondary:
Additional clarification(s):

Drake appears on stage or in the official broadcast or stream; AND

Drake actively and perceptibly contributes to a musical performance by singing (including audible lead or backing vocals), rapping, playing a musical instrument, DJ-ing, or conducting a live band; AND

Is either a scheduled performer, a guest appearance during another artist's set, or is formally announced as a performer by official event organizers or Drake's official representatives.

Note: This Contract may resolve on the announcement of Drake’s anticipated performance at the 2026 FIFA World Cup final halftime show. If an announced Drake later withdraws from the 2026 FIFA World Cup final halftime show, this will not affect the resolution of the Contract.

The following constitutes a performance and IS encompassed by the Payout Criterion:

A full solo performance of one or more songs.

A collaborative or joint performance in which Drake is physically present and actively contributes musically.

A guest appearance during another artist's set in which Drake performs at least one full verse, chorus, or musical segment.

A medley in which Drake is present and actively performing, even if Drake's portion is partial.

A performance in which Drake performs entirely via an officially produced and sanctioned satellite feed, video link, or holographic display that is presented as part of the official live event program, provided that the performance is real-time or was produced specifically for and presented exclusively within the 2026 FIFA World Cup final halftime show.

A DJ set in which Drake is the performing DJ operating equipment live, regardless of whether Drake sings.

A pre-recorded performance segment presented as part of the official event program, provided it is attributed to Drake by the official event organizers and presented as a performance (rather than a tribute, retrospective, or archival clip).

A performance that begins within the official event timeframe but extends beyond it due to scheduling overrun, provided the performance commenced during the official timeframe.

A performance at a rescheduled version of the 2026 FIFA World Cup final halftime show where the reschedule occurs within one year of the original event date, pursuant to the postponement provisions below.
subtitle: Drake
title: Who will perform at the 2026 FIFA World Cup final halftime show?

rules_primary: If USHER performs (or it is announced that they will perform at) at the 2026 FIFA World Cup final halftime show during the official event timeframe, then the market resolves to Yes.
rules_secondary:
Additional clarification(s):

USHER appears on stage or in the official broadcast or stream; AND

USHER actively and perceptibly contributes to a musical performance by singing (including audible lead or backing vocals), rapping, playing a musical instrument, DJ-ing, or conducting a live band; AND

Is either a scheduled performer, a guest appearance during another artist's set, or is formally announced as a performer by official event organizers or USHER's official representatives.

Note: This Contract may resolve on the announcement of USHER’s anticipated performance at the 2026 FIFA World Cup final halftime show. If an announced USHER later withdraws from the 2026 FIFA World Cup final halftime show, this will not affect the resolution of the Contract.

The following constitutes a performance and IS encompassed by the Payout Criterion:

A full solo performance of one or more songs.

A collaborative or joint performance in which USHER is physically present and actively contributes musically.

A guest appearance during another artist's set in which USHER performs at least one full verse, chorus, or musical segment.

A medley in which USHER is present and actively performing, even if USHER's portion is partial.

A performance in which USHER performs entirely via an officially produced and sanctioned satellite feed, video link, or holographic display that is presented as part of the official live event program, provided that the performance is real-time or was produced specifically for and presented exclusively within the 2026 FIFA World Cup final halftime show.

A DJ set in which USHER is the performing DJ operating equipment live, regardless of whether USHER sings.

A pre-recorded performance segment presented as part of the official event program, provided it is attributed to USHER by the official event organizers and presented as a performance (rather than a tribute, retrospective, or archival clip).

A performance that begins within the official event timeframe but extends beyond it due to scheduling overrun, provided the performance commenced during the official timeframe.

A performance at a rescheduled version of the 2026 FIFA World Cup final halftime show where the reschedule occurs within one year of the original event date, pursuant to the postponement provisions below.

rules_primary: If Logic performs (or it is announced that they will perform at) at the 2026 FIFA World Cup final halftime show during the official event timeframe, then the market resolves to Yes.
rules_secondary:
Additional clarification(s):

Logic appears on stage or in the official broadcast or stream; AND

Logic actively and perceptibly contributes to a musical performance by singing (including audible lead or backing vocals), rapping, playing a musical instrument, DJ-ing, or conducting a live band; AND

Is either a scheduled performer, a guest appearance during another artist's set, or is formally announced as a performer by official event organizers or Logic's official representatives.

Note: This Contract may resolve on the announcement of Logic’s anticipated performance at the 2026 FIFA World Cup final halftime show. If an announced Logic later withdraws from the 2026 FIFA World Cup final halftime show, this will not affect the resolution of the Contract.

The following constitutes a performance and IS encompassed by the Payout Criterion:

A full solo performance of one or more songs.

A collaborative or joint performance in which Logic is physically present and actively contributes musically.

A guest appearance during another artist's set in which Logic performs at least one full verse, chorus, or musical segment.

A medley in which Logic is present and actively performing, even if Logic's portion is partial.

A performance in which Logic performs entirely via an officially produced and sanctioned satellite feed, video link, or holographic display that is presented as part of the official live event program, provided that the performance is real-time or was produced specifically for and presented exclusively within the 2026 FIFA World Cup final halftime show.

A DJ set in which Logic is the performing DJ operating equipment live, regardless of whether Logic sings.

A pre-recorded performance segment presented as part of the official event program, provided it is attributed to Logic by the official event organizers and presented as a performance (rather than a tribute, retrospective, or archival clip).

A performance that begins within the official event timeframe but extends beyond it due to scheduling overrun, provided the performance commenced during the official timeframe.

A performance at a rescheduled version of the 2026 FIFA World Cup final halftime show where the reschedule occurs within one year of the original event date, pursuant to the postponement provisions below.
title: Who will perform at the 2026 FIFA World Cup final halftime show?

custom_strike.Entity: Any country not in Europe or South America

early_close_condition: This market will close and expire after the event occurs or the tournament ends.
no_sub_title: Any continent other than Europe or South America to win
rules_primary: If any country not in Europe or South America wins the 2026 Men's FIFA World Cup, then the market resolves to Yes.
rules_secondary:
For settlement purposes, a country is considered part of a continent based on the FIFA World Cup qualification pathway it competes through, rather than strict geographic borders. European nations are those that compete through UEFA qualification, African nations through CAF, North American nations through CONCACAF, South American nations through CONMEBOL, and Asian/Oceanian nations through either AFC or OFC qualification. Refer to the below country designations:
Europe/UEFA: France, Spain, England, Portugal, Germany, Netherlands, Norway, Belgium, Croatia, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Czechia, Switzerland, Türkiye, Sweden, Austria, Scotland
South America/CONMEBOL: Argentina, Brazil, Colombia, Uruguay, Ecuador, Paraguay
North America/CONCACAF: USA, Mexico, Canada, Curaçao, Haiti, Panama
Africa/CAF: Morocco, Congo DR, Senegal, Ivory Coast, Egypt, Ghana, Cape Verde, Algeria, South Africa, Tunisia
Asia or Oceania/AFC or OFC: Japan, Iraq, South Korea, Australia, Iran, Jordan, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, Uzbekistan, New Zealand

This market and these products have not been endorsed by FIFA. Any references to "FIFA", the "FIFA World Cup," or any other associated marks are descriptive only, and do not indicate an endorsement of this product or any affiliation between FIFA and Kalshi.
subtitle: Any continent other than Europe or South America to win
title: Will the winner of the 2026 Men's FIFA World Cup be from any continent other than Europe or South America?
yes_sub_title: Any continent other than Europe or South America to win
parent_event_title: World Cup: Any Continent other than Europe or South America to Win

early_close_condition: This market will close and expire after the event occurs or the tournament ends.
rules_primary: If the longest penalty shootout is at least 6 rounds in the knockout stage of the 2026 Men's FIFA World Cup, then the market resolves to Yes.
rules_secondary:
This market resolves based on the number of rounds in the longest penalty shootout (by number of rounds) in the knockout stage of the 2026 Men's FIFA World Cup. For the purposes of this Contract, a "round" refers to when both teams have each taken one penalty kick (i.e., Round 1: Team A takes their 1st penalty, then Team B takes their 1st penalty. Round 2: Team A takes their 2nd penalty, then Team B takes their 2nd penalty, and so on).

If no matches are decided by penalty shootout in the knockout stage of the 2026 Men's FIFA World Cup, then all markets resolve to No.

This market and these products have not been endorsed by FIFA. Any references to "FIFA", the "FIFA World Cup," or any other associated marks are descriptive only, and do not indicate an endorsement of this product or any affiliation between FIFA and Kalshi.

rules_secondary:
This market resolves based on the number of rounds in the longest penalty shootout (by number of rounds) in the knockout stage of the 2026 Men's FIFA World Cup. For the purposes of this Contract, a "round" refers to when both teams have each taken one penalty kick (i.e., Round 1: Team A takes their 1st penalty, then Team B takes their 1st penalty. Round 2: Team A takes their 2nd penalty, then Team B takes their 2nd penalty, and so on).

If no matches are decided by penalty shootout in the knockout stage of the 2026 Men's FIFA World Cup, then all markets resolve to No.

This market and these products have not been endorsed by FIFA. Any references to "FIFA", the "FIFA World Cup," or any other associated marks are descriptive only, and do not indicate an endorsement of this product or any affiliation between FIFA and Kalshi.

custom_strike.Sporting Outcome: Records a goal in every knockout stage match including the Round of 32, Round of 16, Quarterfinals, Semifinals, and Final

early_close_condition: This market will close and expire after the event occurs or the tournament ends.
no_sub_title: Any player to score a goal in every knockout stage match
rules_primary: If any individual player records a goal in every knockout stage match including the Round of 32, Round of 16, Quarterfinals, Semifinals, and Final in the 2026 Men's FIFA World Cup, then the market resolves to Yes.
rules_secondary:
For the purposes of this market, 'every knockout stage match' refers to all rounds of the knockout stage (excluding the third-place match). The market resolves to Yes if any individual player scores at least one goal in the Round of 32, Round of 16, Quarterfinals, Semifinals, and the Final of the 2026 Men's FIFA World Cup. Therefore, any players on teams that do not reach the Final are not eligible to score a goal in every knockout stage match. Note: Only goals recorded during regulation, stoppage, or extra time count towards this market. Any goals recorded during a penalty shootout do not count. Own goals do not count for purposes of this market.

This market and these products have not been endorsed by FIFA. Any references to "FIFA", the "FIFA World Cup," or any other associated marks are descriptive only, and do not indicate an endorsement of this product or any affiliation between FIFA and Kalshi.

rules_primary: If any country that competes in Europe (UEFA) qualification is the 2026 FIFA Men's World Cup champion, then the market resolves to Yes.
rules_secondary:
For settlement purposes, a country is considered part of a continent based on the FIFA World Cup qualification pathway it competes through, rather than strict geographic borders. European nations are those that compete through UEFA qualification, African nations through CAF, North American nations through CONCACAF, South American nations through CONMEBOL, and Asian/Oceanian nations through either AFC or OFC qualification. This market and these products have not been endorsed by FIFA. Any references to "FIFA", the "FIFA World Cup," or any other associated marks are descriptive only, and do not indicate an endorsement of this product or any affiliation between FIFA and Kalshi. Refer to the below country designations.
Europe/UEFA: France, Spain, England, Portugal, Germany, Netherlands, Norway, Belgium, Croatia, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Czechia, Switzerland, Türkiye, Sweden, Austria, Scotland
South America/CONMEBOL: Argentina, Brazil, Colombia, Uruguay, Ecuador, Paraguay
North America/CONCACAF: USA, Mexico, Canada, Curaçao, Haiti, Panama
Africa/CAF: Morocco, Congo DR, Senegal, Ivory Coast, Egypt, Ghana, Cape Verde, Algeria, South Africa, Tunisia
Asia or Oceania/AFC or OFC: Japan, Iraq, South Korea, Australia, Iran, Jordan, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, Uzbekistan, New Zealand

rules_primary:
If 
the play by play or color commentator(s) says Comeback / Come Back as part of Mexico vs South Africa FIFA World Cup Match
, then the market resolves to Yes.
rules_secondary:
Video of the Mexico vs South Africa FIFA World Cup Match originally scheduled for Jun 11, 2026 will be primarily used to resolve the market; if a consensus by Kalshi employees cannot be reached using video, internal & external (official) transcripts of the Mexico vs South Africa FIFA World Cup Match originally scheduled for Jun 11, 2026 will be used.

Payout Criterion: 
The exact phrase/word, or a plural or possessive form of the phrase/word, must be used. Grammatical/tense inflections are otherwise not included. 
Commentary will count following kickoff and end once the match has concluded, inclusive of extra time and/or a penalty shootout. Commentary will no longer count following the referee's final whistle. If the final action is under VAR review, commentary will end following the referee's decision.
Promotional content aired during the event will count, but advertisements will not. 
Except as otherwise specified, previously aired content, archival footage, and recordings captured at times, places, or events wholly separate from the featured address will not count.
Please see full rules for more details.

Event does not qualify:
If an event is definitively cancelled or the event fails to qualify under the Payout Criterion, or any other circumstance prevents normal resolution, then the market for "Event does not qualify" resolves to Yes and all other markets will resolve to No.  

Postponement:
If the event is postponed, and such a postponement is announced by the end of the following calendar day to be rescheduled to a time within 14 calendar days, the markets shall remain open.

early_close_condition: This market will close and expire early if the event occurs.
rules_primary: If Alisson Becker is selected for the final squad for the Brazil national team for the 2026 FIFA World Cup, then the market resolves to Yes.
rules_secondary:
This market resolves Yes if the player is officially named in Brazil's national team’s final squad for the 2026 World Cup tournament.
All players included on the officially announced final squad list will resolve Yes, regardless of whether they play in any matches.
If a player is selected to the final squad but later withdraws due to injury, suspension, or any other reason, the market will still resolve Yes.
If a player is added to the final squad after the initial announcement but prior to their team’s first match in the tournament, the market will resolve Yes.
Provisional, preliminary, or training camp rosters do not count unless the player is officially included in the final registered squad.

This market and these products have not been endorsed by FIFA. Any references to "FIFA", the "FIFA World Cup," or any other associated marks are descriptive only, and do not indicate an endorsement of this product or any affiliation between FIFA and Kalshi.
subtitle: Alisson Becker

rules_secondary:
This market resolves Yes if the player is officially named in United States' national team’s final squad for the 2026 World Cup tournament.
All players included on the officially announced final squad list will resolve Yes, regardless of whether they play in any matches.
If a player is selected to the final squad but later withdraws due to injury, suspension, or any other reason, the market will still resolve Yes.

Provisional, preliminary, or training camp rosters do not count unless the player is officially included in the final registered squad.

early_close_condition: This market will close and expire early if FIFA officially relocates a U.S.-scheduled match abroad.
rules_primary: If FIFA officially relocates at least one 2026 FIFA World Cup match from a U.S. venue to a venue outside the United States before ||date||, then the market resolves to Yes.
rules_secondary: A relocation requires FIFA to officially move a match from any U.S. venue (including all 50 states, DC, and U.S. territories) to a venue outside the United States. Official relocations are confirmed through FIFA's updated match schedule, official announcements, or the organizing committee's statements. Moving matches between U.S. venues, date/time changes without venue changes, relocations of non-U.S. matches, temporary changes that are reversed, and changes to training facilities do not qualify. If a match begins in the U.S. but is moved abroad mid-match due to disruption, this resolves to Yes. If the entire tournament is cancelled or postponed to 2027+, this resolves to No. If all U.S. matches are relocated abroad, this resolves to Yes.
subtitle: Yes
title: Will any 2026 FIFA World Cup game scheduled in the U.S. be relocated abroad before Jun 11, 2026?
parent_event_title: Will any 2026 World Cup game scheduled in the U.S. be relocated abroad before Jun 11, 2026?

early_close_condition: This market will close and expire early if the event occurs.
rules_primary: If FIFA suspends or ends US involvement in World Cup 2026 (such as moving all of the USA games to Canada and Mexico), then the market resolves to Yes.
title: Will FIFA pull the World Cup out of the USA?
parent_event_title: Will FIFA pull the World Cup out of the USA?

early_close_condition: This market will close and expire early if the executive action is issued.
rules_primary: If Donald Trump has taken any executive action revoking, terminating, or rescinding the suspension of entry for nationals of Haiti, Iran, Côte d'Ivoire, or Senegal before the World Cup, then the market resolves to Yes.
rules_secondary: The qualifying action must be of the specific type designated (executive order, presidential memorandum, proclamation, directive, determination, or finding), be signed by the President personally during the specified time period, and explicitly address the topic in the document's operative provisions, title, or official White House summary. Actions must have legal or policy effect - ceremonial proclamations without policy impact do not qualify unless specifically included. Actions that only incidentally mention the topic, statements without formal action, actions by cabinet members, legislative proposals without executive action, and signing statements do not qualify. The action must be publicly announced or documented by a Source Agency before expiration.
title: Will Donald Trump issue any executive action on revoking, terminating, or rescinding the suspension of entry for nationals of Haiti, Iran, Côte d'Ivoire, or Senegal before the World Cup?
parent_event_title: Will the travel ban end for any countries participating in the World Cup?

early_close_condition: This market will close and expire early if the event occurs.
rules_primary: If any NATO member state boycotts the 2026 USA World Cup before Jun 11, 2026, then the market resolves to Yes.
title: Will any NATO member state boycott the USA World Cup in 2026?
parent_event_title: Will any NATO member state boycott the USA World Cup in 2026?
